title: anettea marshalliana
intro: Anettea marshalliana, also known as the 'tree tobacco' or ' tree nicotine', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant contains high levels of nicotine and other alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves of Anettea marshalliana have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs and colds.
